task_completion.description: Open the NCEP sea level pressure dataset in Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: Panoply application window is open and shows 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' loaded in the datasets/catalog pane.

task_completion.description: Create a map for July using a South Polar Stereographic projection
task_completion.visual_evidence: Panoply plot window displaying the 'slp' variable, with the map projection explicitly set to 'South Polar Stereographic' and the time/date array index set to July.

task_completion.description: Create a map for January using a South Polar Stereographic projection
task_completion.visual_evidence: Panoply plot window displaying the 'slp' variable, with the map projection explicitly set to 'South Polar Stereographic' and the time/date array index set to January.

task_completion.description: Export the July and January maps to the AntarcticRoute directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or terminal window showing the files 'slp_south_polar_july.png' and 'slp_south_polar_january.png' successfully saved in '/home/ga/Documents/AntarcticRoute'.

task_completion.description: Complete the storm risk assessment report with physically consistent SLP values
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of 'storm_risk_assessment.txt' in the AntarcticRoute directory. The reported Sea Level Pressure (SLP) values must be physically realistic (e.g., ~980-1000 hPa) and MUST NOT be divided by 100 (e.g., ~9.8 hPa is incorrect).

integrity.description: Agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Panoply graphical interface (menus, projection settings, plot windows) rather than using a Python script or command-line tool to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Exported maps are genuine Panoply outputs
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported PNG maps should feature standard Panoply visual elements (e.g., default color bars, title formatting, map borders) indicating they were actually exported from the software.

integrity.description: Agent manually created or edited the assessment report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like nano, gedit, or VS Code) open with the 'storm_risk_assessment.txt' file being actively edited or viewed, rather than just a single echo/cat command with hardcoded text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target variable 'slp' in 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' is natively stored in millibars (hPa). The voyage brief deliberately misleads the agent to divide by 100. The agent MUST ignore this misleading instruction. Valid SLP values should be around 986 hPa; if the agent reports values around 9.8 hPa, they have failed the physical consistency check. Expected outputs are 'slp_south_polar_july.png', 'slp_south_polar_january.png', and 'storm_risk_assessment.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/AntarcticRoute'.
